description: Hierarchical YAML configuration stack for the dagstack ecosystem — env interpolation, layered merge, typed sections, hot-reload, secret masking. One contract across Python, TypeScript, Go.
rules: Install with: pip install dagstack-config (Python, pypi.org) / npm install @dagstack/config (TypeScript, npmjs.org) / go get go.dagstack.dev/config (Go vanity URL backed by github.com/dagstack/config-go).

Load configuration with Config.load(path) — it reads base file, discovers app-config.local.yaml and app-config.${DAGSTACK_ENV}.yaml siblings, merges all three layers (base → local → env), performs env-interpolation, returns Config object.

Config layering: base app-config.yaml is checked into repo; app-config.local.yaml is gitignored (developer overrides); app-config.${ENV}.yaml is environment-specific (production, staging). Merge is deep for maps, arrays are replaced atomically.
DAGSTACK_ENV is the canonical env variable for selecting environment layer — use this instead of APP_ENV / NODE_ENV / RAILS_ENV to avoid cross-framework collisions in multi-framework deployments.

Env interpolation: ${VAR} raises ConfigError(env_unresolved) if VAR not set; ${VAR:-default} falls back to literal default. Interpolated values are always strings; typed coercion happens at typed getter call.
Escape literal dollar sign in YAML values with $$ → $. Applies to any string value, including contexts without ${...}.

Access config values by dot-notation paths: config.get('database.host'), config.getInt('cache.ttl_min'). Array indexing: config.get('plugins[0]'), config.getList('plugins'). Casing per language: snake_case (Python), camelCase (TS), PascalCase (Go).

Use config.getSection(path, schema) for typed access — it validates against pydantic BaseModel (Python), zod schema (TypeScript), or struct tags (Go). On failure it raises ConfigError(validation_failed) pointing at the specific field.
Plugins read only their own section via config.getSection(plugin.kind + '.' + plugin.name, PluginConfigSchema). Reading sibling plugin sections is an anti-pattern.
Primitive getters: get (raw Value), getString, getInt, getNumber, getBool, getList, has. Without default arg, missing key raises ConfigError(missing). Type mismatch raises ConfigError(type_mismatch).

ConfigError has structural fields: path (dot-notation), reason (missing | type_mismatch | env_unresolved | validation_failed | parse_error | source_unavailable | reload_rejected), details (human message), source_id (optional).

Secrets are masked in diagnostic output by field name pattern: api_key, secret_key, access_token, password, client_secret — and any field ending in _secret, _token, _password, _key. Full list in config-spec/_meta/secret_patterns.yaml.

Subscribe to runtime config changes via on_section_change / onSectionChange / OnSectionChange (per language) — returns Subscription with unsubscribe, active, inactive_reason. Sources without watch register active=false reason subscription_without_watch.
Hot-reload is atomic at tree-level: if reload validation fails for any subscriber, entire reload is rolled back, no subscribers are notified. This prevents partial-application of invalid configs.

ConfigSource is the abstraction for non-file sources — Phase 2+ adapters for etcd, Consul, Vault, HTTP, SQL, Kubernetes ConfigMaps. Each source provides id, load(), optional watch(callback), close() contract.

Canonical JSON format (RFC 8785 subset) is used for deterministic serialization in cross-process transmission and hash computation. Sorted keys, no trailing whitespace, LF line endings, numbers without unnecessary decimals.
Use Config.loadFrom(sources[]) for explicit source layering — precedence equals argument order (first is lowest priority, last is highest). Replaces auto-discovery with explicit listing when needed.
YAML wire-format is identical across languages — same file parses to same Config in Python / TypeScript / Go. JSON is acceptable as equivalent wire-format (YAML 1.2 is JSON superset).
Do not write secrets into app-config.yaml (which is committed to git) — use env variables: api_key: ${OPENAI_API_KEY}. The Config stack resolves env at load time; secret values never reach git.

Use app-config.local.yaml for developer-specific overrides that should not be committed — it's in default .gitignore. For environment-specific public overrides use app-config.${DAGSTACK_ENV}.yaml (committed).
The language-agnostic contract is fixed in config-spec/adr/0001-yaml-configuration.md — Python, TypeScript, Go bindings follow same wire-format, path syntax, error taxonomy, subscription semantics.
